purpose: Define the sanitized evidence needed to prove uploads, generated run artifacts, account exports, deletion requests, and review demo data survive API and worker restarts before public launch or real video generation.

officialReferences.name: Docker volumes
officialReferences.whyItMatters: Container-generated data should be written to managed volumes instead of relying on a container writable layer.

officialReferences.name: Kubernetes volumes
officialReferences.whyItMatters: Persistent volumes can exist beyond a pod lifecycle, while ephemeral volumes are tied to pod lifetime.

officialReferences.name: Google Cloud Run container filesystem
officialReferences.whyItMatters: Some serverless container filesystems are in-memory and do not persist when an instance stops.

officialReferences.name: Amazon S3 object storage
officialReferences.whyItMatters: Object storage is a candidate adapter for durable uploads, generated outputs, exports, and backup/restore workflows.

acceptedShapes: object storage bucket plus server-side adapter
managed persistent disk mounted into API and worker services

storageTargets.exampleEvidence: A demo product image remains listed and readable after restart.
A dry-run or generated result remains visible in task/result cards after restart.
A review-account data export request can be downloaded or inspected after restart.
A deletion request record remains queued or completed after restart.
Seeded demo product, account asset, and dry-run task remain available to the review account.

preRestart: Confirm RUN_STORAGE_DIR points to a production persistent path or object-storage adapter.
Log in with a review-safe account.
Create one data export request and one deletion request record.
restartActions: Do not clear the mounted volume or object storage bucket.
postRestart: Log in again or verify the session state.
Confirm uploaded product image is still readable.
Confirm dry-run task/result is still visible.
Confirm data export and deletion request records still exist.
Run npm run cloud:smoke against https://www.zkraiflow.top.
Record only redacted paths and object ids.

evidenceRequired: RUN_STORAGE_DIR value is non-empty and not a temporary path
storage target type is mounted-volume or object-storage-adapter
path or bucket identifier is redacted if private
no private customer image is used
dry-run or generated result exists before restart
backup owner is named outside placeholder fields
retention and deletion policy is recorded

closeoutGates: Production RUN_STORAGE_DIR or object storage adapter is selected and configured.
Uploads survive API and worker restart.
Generated run/task artifacts survive API and worker restart.
Data export and deletion request records survive restart.
Backup frequency, owner, and retention/deletion policy are recorded.
npm run storage:restart-template passes before collecting production evidence.
release-blockers-register keeps persistent-storage blocked until filled restart evidence exists.
